title: Candor
description: Anonymous feedback platform that uses AI to moderate messages and turn your inbox into a trackable growth plan, with a real-time inbox.
overview: Candor lets anyone collect anonymous feedback through a personal link — but instead of stopping at "you got a message," it uses an AI coach to turn that feedback into a structured, trackable growth plan. It ships with a credit economy, real-time updates, AI moderation, and a full admin console.
problem: Most anonymous-message apps dump feedback on you and stop there — no moderation, no structure, and no way to actually act on what you hear. Honest feedback is valuable, but raw and unorganized it rarely turns into real change.
solution: Candor treats feedback as the start of a loop, not the end. Incoming messages are screened by Gemini for abuse, then an AI coach clusters your inbox into themes and drafts a concrete growth plan that flows through To Do → In Progress → Completed — every task mapped back to the exact feedback that inspired it. An idempotent credit ledger and per-user AI quotas keep the AI usage sustainable, and a real-time inbox streams new messages the moment they arrive.
features: Anonymous messaging through a shareable personal link, with an accept-messages toggle
Real-time inbox streamed via Server-Sent Events backed by MongoDB change streams
AI safety moderation (Gemini) that flags abusive content before it reaches you
AI Feedback Coach that clusters messages into themes and drafts a trackable growth board
Idempotent, append-only credit ledger with per-user daily AI quotas and graceful degradation
NextAuth (email + password and Google OAuth) with email verification and password reset
Admin console — moderation queue, user management, roadmap editor, and full audit log
Production-grade SEO, server-side rate limiting, and Zod validation shared across client and API

architecture.summary: A Next.js App Router app where API routes stream a real-time inbox over Server-Sent Events (backed by MongoDB change streams) and call Google Gemini for AI moderation and coaching, with Upstash Redis enforcing per-user AI quotas and rate limits.

title: SQLPlay
tagline: An in-browser SQL playground, published on npm
description: A zero-setup SQL playground that runs a real SQLite database entirely in your browser via WebAssembly. Practice SQL with no server and no install — published on npm.
overview: SQLPlay is an H2-Console-style SQL playground for JavaScript. It runs a real in-memory SQLite database — compiled to WebAssembly via sql.js — entirely in the browser, so anyone can practice SQL with zero backend and zero setup. It's published on npm and launches with a single `npx sqlplay`.
problem: Java developers have the H2 Console for quickly practicing SQL against a throwaway database. JavaScript had no comparable zero-setup equivalent — you either installed a database locally or signed up for an online sandbox. There was a clear gap for an instant, local, no-install SQL playground.
solution: SQLPlay puts a friendly console UI on top of sql.js (SQLite compiled to WebAssembly), so a full SQL database runs client-side in the browser. It ships as an npm package you launch with `npx sqlplay`, loads sample datasets, and offers a query editor, schema browser, ER diagram, and CSV export — all with no server involved.
features: A real in-memory SQLite database running 100% in the browser via WebAssembly (sql.js)
Zero setup — launch instantly with `npx sqlplay`, or use the hosted live demo
Query editor with syntax highlighting and keyboard-run (Ctrl / ⌘ + Enter)
Schema browser, ER-diagram view, and an interactive data editor
Bundled sample datasets plus SQL challenges to practice against
Import your own data and export results as CSV or a .sqlite file
Published and versioned on npm for anyone to install and run

architecture.summary: SQLPlay runs entirely in the browser — there is no server. The React UI drives sql.js (SQLite compiled to WebAssembly), which runs a full in-memory SQLite database client-side. The npm CLI simply serves the static app locally.

title: InsightBlog
description: A developer publishing platform with an AI writing assistant, a multi-source trending hub, and built-in creator analytics dashboards.
overview: InsightBlog is a publishing platform built to feel like a production SaaS product rather than a Medium clone. Beyond writing and publishing, it helps creators discover trending topics, improve drafts with AI, and understand exactly how their content performs.
problem: A basic blog app is just CRUD. Real creators need more: to know what to write about, to improve their drafts, and to understand what actually lands with readers — capabilities that generic blogging tools simply don't provide.
solution: A domain-driven, serverless platform delivered across five phases. The backend runs on Hono + Prisma + Neon Postgres on Cloudflare Workers, with JWT auth (access/refresh, RBAC), a full blog lifecycle, and a social graph. An AI assistant helps improve writing, suggests SEO and topics, and produces a grounded engagement forecast — a heuristic over readability and history rather than fabricated predictions — with a graceful mock fallback when AI is unavailable.
features: Secure auth — JWT access/refresh tokens, RBAC, email verification, and password reset
Full blog lifecycle — draft, schedule, publish, version history, categories, and image upload
Social graph — comments, likes, bookmarks, follows, notifications, and recommendations
AI assistant — improve-writing, SEO and topic suggestions, cover ideas, and engagement forecast
Multi-source Trending Hub surfacing what to write next
Author and admin analytics dashboards with charts
Shared Zod DTOs across client and server in an npm-workspaces monorepo

architecture.summary: A domain-driven, serverless request flow running on Cloudflare's edge. The React SPA calls a Hono API on Cloudflare Workers, which reaches Neon PostgreSQL through Prisma Accelerate (connection pooling) and calls Google Gemini for the AI writing assistant.

title: WoCo PMS
description: Multi-tenant HR platform for performance reviews — goal & KPI tracking, 360° feedback, cycle timelines, and AI-generated appraisal questions.
overview: WoCo PMS is a multi-tenant HR performance-management platform used by client organizations to run their entire review process — a React SPA backed by a Node/TypeScript API. It replaces slow, spreadsheet-driven annual reviews with a continuous, real-time performance system.
problem: Traditional performance reviews are annual, manual, and disconnected — HR teams juggle spreadsheets for goals, 360° feedback, and appraisals across many client organizations, with no single source of truth and no real-time visibility.
solution: A multi-tenant platform that runs the full review cycle in one place: goal and KPI tracking, 360° feedback with reviewer-visibility rules, per-cycle performance timelines, and an AI question generator (OpenAI) that drafts weightage-balanced KPI, 360, competency, and PLI questions. Each tenant is isolated with its own data, and a bulk Excel pipeline lets HR import questions at scale.
features: Goal & KPI tracking across review cycles
360° feedback with configurable reviewer-visibility rules
Per-cycle performance timelines and evaluation windows
AI question generator (OpenAI gpt-4.1-mini) for KPI/KRA, 360, competency, and PLI questions
Bulk Excel upload for question authoring at scale
Star-rating system with scale normalization across the app
Multi-tenant architecture with per-tenant data isolation and S3 document uploads

architecture.summary: A multi-tenant SaaS: three React SPAs (Employee, Manager, HR) talk to a layered Node/Express API, which serves data from MongoDB, MySQL, and Redis and calls OpenAI for AI features. High-level overview.

contribution: Core engineer and #1 frontend contributor — integrated 100+ APIs, built 200+ reusable MUI components, and implemented Redux Toolkit state management
Led a backend refactoring & performance initiative that raised the codebase's documented quality score from 4.5 to 8.6/10
Cut authenticated-request overhead ~145ms → ~35ms (~4× faster) with a fail-open Redis cache, and added 44 MongoDB indexes for 100–500× faster indexed queries
Built the AI question-generator UI and the bulk Excel-upload flow, and hardened the platform — closing 10 SQL-injection points and fixing 25+ critical bugs
